articles.title: Trending Stocks Today | Binah Capital Group Soars 53.23% Pre-Market
articles.summary: Binah Capital Group (BCG.US) saw a significant pre-market surge of 53.23% to $3.08. Other top gainers included PMGC Holdings (ELAB.US) and Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI.US), while Nike (NKE.US) and Occidental Petroleum (OXY.US) were among the pre-market losers. The trending stocks are identified based on capitalization, turnover ratio, and percentage change, with different criteria for small-cap and large-cap stocks.

articles.title: Golf Apparel Market Is Going to Boom | Nike • Adidas • Under Armour • Puma • Callaway

articles.title: Lululemon convinces US judge to overturn Nike shoe patent verdict
articles.summary: A federal judge in Manhattan has overturned a jury's verdict that Lululemon infringed a Nike shoe patent, invalidating the patent and reversing a $355,450 award against Lululemon. Nike had sued Lululemon in 2023, claiming its shoes infringed two patents related to manufacturing techniques, but the judge ruled the asserted patent was invalid due to obviousness. A separate patent infringement case by Nike against Lululemon concerning its Mirror Home Gym is still ongoing.

A separate patent infringement case by Nike against Lululemon concerning its Mirror Home Gym is still ongoing.

articles.title: Earnings Flash (NKE) NIKE, Inc. Reports Q3 Revenue $11.28B, vs. FactSet Est of $11.23B
articles.summary: NIKE, Inc. reported its Q3 revenue at $11.28 billion, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $11.23 billion. Despite beating revenue expectations, several analysts have adjusted their ratings and price targets for Nike, citing a slower than planned pace of recovery and delayed sales. The stock has plummeted to its lowest in a decade, influenced by broader market concerns and hopes for an end to the Iran war.

The stock has plummeted to its lowest in a decade, influenced by broader market concerns and hopes for an end to the Iran war.

articles.title: NIKE, Inc. Reports Fiscal 2026 Third Quarter Results
articles.summary: NIKE, Inc. reported its fiscal 2026 third-quarter results, with revenues flat at $11.3 billion on a reported basis and down 3% currency-neutral, and diluted earnings per share at $0.35, a 35% decrease. The company highlighted actions taken to improve business health, with efforts to drive momentum in prioritized areas despite declines in EMEA and Greater China. Despite challenges, management expressed confidence in profitable long-term growth and its track record of shareholder returns, including consistent dividend payouts.

Despite challenges, management expressed confidence in profitable long-term growth and its track record of shareholder returns, including consistent dividend payouts.

articles.title: McCormick, Allbirds And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Tuesday
articles.summary: This article highlights several stocks to watch, including McCormick & Company Inc. and Allbirds Inc., ahead of Tuesday's market open. McCormick is expected to report its quarterly earnings, while Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc. announced mixed fourth-quarter results and progress on its new SpaceShip. Allbirds shares surged after the company announced an agreement to sell most of its intellectual property, and TD Synnex Corp. and Nike Inc. are also expected to release earnings.

articles.title: Profit drops at NIKE (NYSE: NKE) despite flat Q3 2026 sales
articles.summary: NIKE, Inc. reported mixed fiscal Q3 2026 results with revenue remaining flat at $11.3 billion but a significant 35% drop in net income to $520 million and diluted earnings per share to $0.35. This decline was primarily attributed to lower gross margins, driven by higher North America tariffs, and an increased effective tax rate. The company also saw uneven performance across brands and channels, with wholesale revenue rising but NIKE Direct and Converse revenues falling.

articles.title: Nike Delivers Q3 Earnings Beat As Turnaround Progresses, Shares Slide
articles.summary: Nike Inc. reported better-than-expected third-quarter fiscal 2026 results with revenues of $11.28 billion and earnings of 35 cents per share. Despite the earnings beat and flat year-over-year revenue growth, Nike's shares slid in after-hours trading. The company emphasized ongoing efforts to improve business health and progress in its turnaround strategy.
